items.title: Why Apple Sued OpenAI, New York Takes on Data Centers, and What to Know about Cyclosporiasis
items.summary: On today’s Uncanny Valley, we unpack OpenAI’s ongoing drama, both legal and reputational, and whether these developments could further hurt the company—particularly in its fight against Anthropic.

items.title: Salad Chains Are Seeing Foot Traffic Drop Over Cyclosporiasis Fears
items.summary: Foot traffic to leafy green chains is falling, data shows. Still, a few brave souls who spoke to WIRED were determined to get their fix. “I honestly didn’t even think about” the risk of explosive diarrhea, one says.

items.title: Do Face Masks Help With Wildfire Smoke? Yes, But More Is Needed
items.summary: As wildfire smoke threatens air quality and safety for millions, here’s your playbook to keep your family safe from the damaging effects of smoke.

items.title: Coca-Cola suspended production at its Fairlife dairy after a ransomware attack
items.summary: Coca Cola said dairy production at its Fairlife unit will "remain suspended" in the United States following a hack.

items.title: Founders Fund hires former OpenAI exec Ryan Beiermeister (and not because of her ‘Mafia’ skills)
items.summary: Ryan Beiermeister, who demonstrated cool analysis in the Founders Fund YouTube series "Mafia," has joined the firm as a partner.

items.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
items.summary: Google is adding personalized AI avatars to Vids that let users create videos starring a digital version of themselves, alongside Gemini Omni-powered tools for generating and editing videos from prompts and reference images.

items.title: Blasts reported in Iran as US launches new wave of strikes
items.summary: The US says its attacks aim to "further degrade Iranian military capabilities", as fighting continued for the sixth day in a row.

items.title: Zelensky's removal of popular defence minister sparks protests in Ukraine
items.summary: A rift between Fedorov and Commander-in-Chief Oleksandr Syrskyi was apparently at the heart of Zelensky's decision.

items.title: Teenager accused of carrying out sabotage actions in Poland for Russia
items.summary: Polish security services say the aim was to incite ethnic tensions between Poland and Ukraine.

items.title: Dallas Fed President Logan calls for 'modestly' higher interest rates
items.summary: The policymaker said this week's good inflation news wasn't good enough.

items.title: Short sellers load up against SpaceX as stock drops below IPO price
items.summary: About 185 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 29% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

items.title: Sen. Warren says Trump's CFPB overhaul has cost Americans $26.5 billion
items.summary: Sen. Elizabeth Warren says the Trump administration's rollback of CFPB rules and enforcement has cost Americans up to $26.5 billion so far.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: White House revives rule to deny green cards to migrants who use safety net programs
items.summary: The Trump administration is reviving a policy that would allow immigration officers to deny green cards to applicants who use public benefits like Medicaid, housing assistance or food stamps.   The policy governs how officials can apply the “public charge” test, which is used to determine whether applicants are likely to rely on government support. …

items.title: Wall Street traders can soon pay for early looks at Truth Social posts
items.summary: President Trump’s social media platform announced plans Thursday to sell Wall Street firms early access to its “most market-moving” posts. Trump Media & Technology Group (TMTG), the parent company of Truth Social, said it would launch “Truth API” — a direct data feed that would give investment firms “real-time access to posts from the highest-ranking…

items.title: More than 400 sick as CDC searches for the source of a mystery outbreak
items.summary: A growing Cyclospora outbreak has sickened more than 400 people in four states, and investigators are still searching for the contaminated food responsible. The CDC warns the actual number of cases is likely much higher and urges anyone with symptoms to seek medical care.

items.title: Quantum breakthrough links light and magnetism in atomically thin materials
items.summary: A new review highlights exciting progress in atomically thin quantum materials where light and magnetism work together in ways never before possible. In these materials, light-generated excitons can interact directly with magnetic behavior, creating opportunities to control magnetic states using light alone. Scientists believe this could pave the way for advanced optical memory, quantum devices, and ultra-efficient photonic technologies.

items.title: The hidden skeleton “gatekeeper” inside brain cells could help fight Alzheimer's
items.summary: Researchers have discovered that a microscopic skeleton inside neurons does much more than hold cells together. It acts as a gatekeeper that controls what brain cells absorb and when they absorb it. When this protective structure weakens, neurons rapidly take in harmful proteins associated with Alzheimer's disease, suggesting that stabilizing it could become a promising new strategy for preventing brain cell damage.

items.title: This “exotic weirdo” exoplanet has a rocky surface and an atmosphere
items.summary: Astronomers found a helium atmosphere around a rocky exoplanet — a good sign for the search for life outside the solar system.

items.title: Genes offer new clues to stopping Huntington’s disease in its tracks
items.summary: A new generation of therapies aims to intervene at a recently discovered gap between the disease’s molecular march and its neurological consequences.

items.title: An ancient Maya astronomer finally has a name
items.summary: A Maya calendar formula bears the name Sak Tahn Waax, the first known Classic Maya mathematician-astronomer directly credited for such work.
